title: LiveComponent IDs must be stable when the component should persist
problem: Deriving a LiveComponent id from changing data (e.g. id={"panel-#{@selected_item.id}"}) causes Phoenix to destroy and remount the component instead of calling update/2. Shows stale content or fails to refresh.
fix: Use a stable id (id="panel") when the component should persist across assign changes. Reserve dynamic ids for cases where you intentionally want a fresh instance per item.

title: Reusable components should not embed navigation behavior
problem: Wrapping component content in <.link navigate={...}> prevents reuse in contexts needing different click behavior. The navigate fires before phx-click reaches the server.
fix: Make reusable display components pure presentation (div wrappers, no click behavior). Let each call site decide: <.link navigate> for navigation, <button phx-click> for events.

title: Partitioned queries with implicit exclusion silently drop records
problem: Splitting related records across multiple specialized queries makes records invisible if they don't match either query. Changing external state silently hides previously visible records.
fix: Use a single comprehensive query that returns all records, then group/partition in memory. Test each partition bucket explicitly.

title: Batch imports must be idempotent and categorize failures
problem: (1) Printing only aggregate skip counts makes diagnosis impossible — duplicates vs missing references need different responses. (2) Re-running imports crashes on unique constraint if changeset lacks unique_constraint/3.
fix: Categorize failures into expected skips (summary count) and real errors (individual lines with context). Always add unique_constraint to changesets for unique DB indexes. For idempotent imports: look up by external ID first — unchanged=skip, changed=update, missing=create. Use find-or-create for parent entities.

title: Page layout should be full-width with scoped scroll containers
problem: Adding overflow-y-auto or max-h-screen to content containers breaks natural page scroll. Page-level horizontal scroll is jarring.
fix: Pages go edge-to-edge, no padding on <main>. Use overflow-x-hidden on outer wrapper, overflow-x-auto only on containers whose content may exceed viewport width. Let vertical scroll be page-level.

title: Test output must be clean — no log errors or warnings
problem: Noisy test output ([error], [warning], 404s) hides real problems. Common cause: factories create DB records with status:processed but no actual files on disk.
fix: Verify with mix test 2>&1 | grep "\[error\]" before considering a task complete. Ensure test records referencing files have those files where the server expects them.

title: Sorting by input field is not the same as sorting by computed result
problem: Finding max of a derived value (e.g. age = end - start) by sorting one input only works if the other input is constant. Fails when both inputs vary.
fix: Load all candidates, compute the derived value for each in application code, then pick the max.

title: Functions that accept nil to branch behavior are an anti-pattern
problem: A function using nil to switch between fundamentally different behaviors hides intent and makes call sites ambiguous.
fix: Create two functions with descriptive names that each do one thing. Let the caller choose explicitly. Pattern matching for dispatch is fine, but distinct behaviors need distinct public API names. Separate functions are easier to test individually.

title: Event handlers must update all dependent assigns
problem: Modifying state in handle_event that other assigns depend on won't trigger handle_params (which only runs on mount/URL changes). Dependent UI won't update until navigation.
fix: After modifying state in handle_event, also update derived assigns. Extract shared derivation logic to a private function callable from both handle_params and handle_event.

title: JS hook pushEvent sends native types, phx-value-* sends strings
problem: pushEvent({id: 42}) arrives as integer 42. Calling String.to_integer(42) raises ArgumentError. phx-value-* always arrives as strings via DOM serialization.
fix: Normalize in the JS hook — always send string values (String(person.id)) to match phx-value-* convention, so all event handlers can use String.to_integer/1 consistently.

title: Colocated JS hooks resolve under OTP app name, not project directory name
problem: Import in app.js must use the OTP app name (phoenix-colocated/ancestry), not the directory name. Mismatch causes hooks to silently fail at runtime with no build errors.
fix: Define hooks as plain JS objects in assets/js/, import in app.js, pass to LiveSocket hooks option. This avoids the colocated hooks build pipeline entirely.

title: Use safe DOM methods instead of string-based HTML in JS hooks
problem: String-based HTML construction in hooks (even with DB-sourced data) triggers security warnings and risks XSS.
fix: Use document.createElement, element.textContent, element.appendChild. For clearing containers use element.replaceChildren().

title: Expensive queries in mount cause DB connection race conditions in tests
problem: Sequential DB queries in mount/3 hold the sandbox connection. If the test process exits before mount completes, the connection owner dies producing owner exited errors.
fix: Use assign_async/3 for expensive or non-critical data loading. When re-assigning synchronously elsewhere, wrap with Phoenix.LiveView.AsyncResult.ok(value).

title: SVG currentColor can be invisible if text color matches background
problem: stroke="currentColor" inherits from container's text-* class. If text color matches background, SVG renders but is invisible. DOM tests won't catch it.
fix: Verify SVG color classes against actual background. Use a contrasting color (e.g. text-base-content/30). Test visibility visually — structural/DOM tests won't catch invisible-but-present elements.

title: JS hooks must handle all data attribute variants they consume
problem: Adding a new data-* variant on the server without updating the JS hook's dispatch logic causes the new variant to silently fall through to a default.
fix: When adding a new data attribute variant server-side, grep JS hooks that consume it and add matching handlers. Add a comment in JS listing all expected prefixes.

title: Use router-level on_mount hooks for tenant enforcement
problem: Placing on_mount hooks in individual LiveView modules creates boilerplate and makes hook ordering unreliable. Sandbox hook must run before tenant hook.
fix: Register on_mount hooks at live_session level in the router. List sandbox hook first, then tenant hook. LiveView modules just access socket.assigns.organization.

title: Use phx-click-away instead of parent phx-click for dropdowns
problem: phx-click on a parent to implement click-away causes event bubbling — clicks on child elements (search inputs) close the dropdown immediately.
fix: Use phx-click-away on the dropdown element itself. For autofocusing dynamic inputs, use phx-mounted={JS.focus()} instead of HTML autofocus.

title: Checkbox phx-click sends empty params on uncheck
problem: Unchecking a checkbox with phx-click + phx-value-value sends %{} not %{"value" => "false"}. Pattern matching on %{"value" => value} crashes with FunctionClauseError.
fix: Accept any params and use bracket access: params["value"] == "true". nil == "true" evaluates to false, correctly representing unchecked.

title: Layout component attrs must be explicitly passed from every call site
problem: Omitting an attr declared with default: nil on Layouts.app silently gets nil instead of the socket assign. Conditional rendering blocks fail silently.
fix: After adding a new attr to a shared layout component, grep all call sites and ensure every one passes the attr. Socket assigns are not automatically forwarded to component attrs.

title: Toolbar actions belong on desktop only — mobile reaches them through the nav drawer
problem: Rendering toolbar action buttons on every breakpoint causes them to overflow and wrap on narrow screens, especially with multi-word labels. Pages that diverge from the project's toolbar pattern (different padding, always-visible actions) create a janky inconsistent mobile UX.
fix: Pick one toolbar pattern and apply it everywhere. Wrap the action group in hidden lg:flex so it only renders on desktop. Expose the same actions on mobile through a parallel page_actions slot inside the nav drawer. Standardize toolbar padding (one value across all pages). When adding a new page, copy an existing page's toolbar structure rather than inventing a new one.

title: Slide-out drawer actions that change the main canvas must dismiss the drawer
problem: When a control inside a slide-out drawer or sidebar fires a server event but doesn't close the drawer, the resulting state change is invisible — the canvas updates beneath a drawer that still covers it. Especially easy to miss when a component is shared between a desktop side panel and a mobile drawer instance, because the desktop version has nothing to close.
fix: Chain the drawer-close JS command with the server push in the markup: phx-click={toggle_drawer() |> JS.push("event", value: %{...})}. For components shared between a permanent panel and a drawer, parameterize the close behavior with a flag so each call site opts in independently. The drawer instance closes; the panel instance does not.

title: Auth scaffolders ship defaults — audit them against your threat model
problem: Generated auth modules (phx.gen.auth, devise, etc.) ship with defensive defaults like sudo-mode (re-auth within N minutes for sensitive routes). For low-stakes apps these defaults add friction that outweighs the protection — users hit re-login prompts during normal use. Conversely, accepting defaults blindly on a high-stakes app may leave gaps the scaffold didn't anticipate.
fix: Read every default the auth scaffold applies to authenticated routes and decide explicitly whether each one fits the threat model. When you weaken a default (e.g. swapping require_sudo_mode for require_authenticated), document the decision in a learning so it isn't silently reintroduced when the auth module is regenerated or copied to a new project. When you accept a default, also document it so the next person doesn't need to re-derive the reasoning.

title: Pre-collect side effects, mutate DB, then clean up only on commit
problem: When deleting a DB record that owns external resources (uploaded files on S3, queue entries, cache keys), the naive ordering — clean up the resources first, then delete the row — leaves the system in an unrecoverable state if the DB delete fails. The DB rolls back but the files are already gone. Conversely, putting the cleanup inside the same transaction or after the cascade FK fires usually doesn't work either: cascade deletes via FK ON DELETE CASCADE bypass any application-level callback, so per-row cleanup hooks (e.g. Waffle delete) are silently skipped.
fix: Three-phase pattern: (1) Preload the parent and walk every association that owns external resources, building an in-memory manifest of {kind, struct} entries plus any local directories. (2) Run the DB delete in a single transaction. The FK cascade handles all child rows. (3) Only on the success branch, walk the manifest and call the appropriate cleanup function for each entry. On the error branch, do nothing — the manifest is discarded and the resources are still intact. This is the only ordering that gives both atomicity (failed DB leaves resources alone) and completeness (cascaded children's resources still get cleaned up). Use a single polymorphic cleanup function dispatching on tagged tuples ({:photo, ...}, {:cover, ...}, {:person_photo, ...}) so adding new resource kinds is a one-clause change. Best-effort cleanup of individual entries (try/rescue + log) keeps one corrupt resource from breaking the whole batch.

title: JS hook destroyed() must guard against state mounted() may not have set
problem: A JS hook's mounted() callback may short-circuit conditionally (viewport check, feature detection, async failure) and return without assigning instance fields. The destroyed() callback then runs unconditionally and references those undefined fields, throwing a TypeError that corrupts LiveView's hook teardown lifecycle. The visible symptom is usually downstream: subsequent clicks on unrelated elements stop working, even though the failing hook has nothing to do with them. The thrown error is invisible unless the dev console is open.
fix: Guard destroyed() against the field that mounted() assigns first. Pattern: `if (!this.criticalField) return` at the top of destroyed(). The guard makes the destroy path a no-op when mounted() short-circuited, restoring the hook to a benign no-op for that platform. Apply this defensively to every hook that has any conditional bail-out in mounted().

title: Playwright considers transform-translated elements visible
problem: Slide-in drawers and sidebars often use CSS transforms (`transform: translateX(-100%)` then `translate-x-0`) to animate in and out. Playwright's default visibility heuristic checks for non-zero bounding box and `display !== 'none'` — translated-off-screen elements still satisfy both conditions, so `assert_has` and `refute_has` will both report the drawer's contents as visible regardless of state. Tests that try to detect 'drawer is open' by asserting on a row inside it will pass even when the drawer is closed.
fix: Detect drawer state by class-attribute matching on the drawer's open/closed class, not by descendant visibility. Use a CSS attribute selector with `~=` (whole-word match) to avoid partial collisions: `aside#nav-drawer[class~='translate-x-0']` matches the open state without also matching `-translate-x-full`. The same applies to any modal/overlay/sidebar that animates via transform. Note this in test comments so the next maintainer doesn't 'fix' the selector to a more intuitive shape.

title: Use descriptive FK column names over generic entity_id
problem: When a foreign key represents a specific role or relationship (e.g., the account that created a record), using the generic entity name (account_id) loses the intent. A table with multiple FKs to the same entity (e.g., created_by, updated_by, assigned_to) would all collide as account_id. Even with a single FK, account_id does not communicate whether this is the creator, owner, assignee, or something else.
fix: Name FK columns after the role they represent: created_by instead of account_id, assigned_to instead of user_id, approved_by instead of approver_id. The column name should answer "what is this reference for?" not "what table does it point to?". Reserve the generic entity_id pattern for join tables or cases where the relationship truly is generic (e.g., a polymorphic association).

title: Template struct field access is a runtime-only failure — compile and unit tests miss it
problem: HEEx templates access struct fields dynamically at runtime. Using a wrong field name (e.g. .file instead of .image on a Photo schema) compiles without error and passes unit tests that exercise context logic but never render the template. The KeyError only surfaces when a user navigates to the page with real data. This is especially common with Waffle upload fields where the field name (e.g. :image, :photo, :cover) varies by schema and is not obvious from the uploader module name.
fix: Always verify schema field names by reading the schema file before referencing them in templates — never guess. Add at least one LiveView rendering test (live/2 + assert rendered content) per new page that exercises the main data path with factory-created records. For pages displaying preloaded associations, the test must insert associated records (e.g. a memory with a cover_photo) so the template branch that renders the association is exercised.
